title: Peanut & Sweet Potato Stew

overview.description: A hearty West African-inspired stew of sweet potatoes, black-eyed beans and peanut butter simmered with tomatoes and warming spices.

method: Heat the oil in a large saucepan over a medium heat.
Cook the onion for 6–8 minutes until softened.
Add the garlic and ginger and cook for 1 minute.
Stir in the cumin, coriander and smoked paprika and cook for 30 seconds.
Add the chopped tomatoes and cook for 5 minutes.
Stir in the sweet potatoes, black-eyed beans and vegetable stock.
Bring to the boil, then reduce to a gentle simmer.
Cook for 20–25 minutes until the sweet potatoes are tender.
Stir the peanut butter into a little hot cooking liquid until smooth, then return it to the pan.
Add the spinach and cook until just wilted.
Season with salt and black pepper.
Scatter with chopped coriander before serving.

learning.did-you-know: Groundnuts (peanuts) feature prominently in many West African cuisines, where they're used to enrich soups, stews and sauces.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How peanut butter can thicken and enrich a stew, and how balancing sweet vegetables with savoury spices creates depth of flavour.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: An inexpensive, nutritious vegetarian meal that introduces West African flavours while making excellent use of pantry ingredients.
chapman-tips: Use smooth peanut butter with as few ingredients as possible.
Mix the peanut butter with a little hot stock before adding it to prevent lumps.
The stew thickens as it cools and often tastes even better the following day.
notes.batch-cooking: Freeze in individual portions for quick weekday meals.
notes.leftovers: Excellent reheated and also works well served over rice the next day.
